refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from mission context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation—the act of translating mission inputs into performance targets. Recording and managing are secondary concerns that belong to separate atomic FRs. This refinement isolates the core problem: given worksite inputs (mission, constraints, safety plan, operational policies), produce a set of performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable and justified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system accepts worksite mission context inputs (e.g., task type, terrain, payload, safety constraints) and produces a documented set of performance targets (e.g., cycle time ≤ X min, energy efficiency ≥ Y kWh/ton, availability ≥ Z%) with explicit traceability to input parameters. Acceptance: performance targets are mathematically or logically justified from mission inputs and reviewed by Site Manager and Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements with traceability metadata (source inputs, derivation date, responsible stakeholder).
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall enable performance requirement updates in response to worksite condition changes or operational policy revisions.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Establish a parameterized mission-context intake process that captures worksite operational profile (hazard class, duty cycle, environmental conditions, safety constraints) and maps each input to a corresponding performance target via a documented traceability matrix, with version control for baseline snapshots.

DP-B: Implement a hierarchical performance requirement derivation engine that accepts worksite mission inputs, applies a rule-based inference system to generate performance targets, and maintains an audit log of all derivations with source traceability.

DP-C: Design a manual performance specification workflow where domain experts review worksite mission context, author performance targets in a centralized specification document, and record calibration/baseline updates through a change-control process.

DP-D: Create a hybrid intake-and-inference architecture: capture mission context via structured intake forms, apply domain-specific heuristics to propose candidate targets, require expert review and approval, and persist all versions in a versioned repository with full traceability links.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Create a hybrid intake-and-inference architecture: capture mission context via structured intake forms, apply domain-specific heuristics to propose candidate targets, require expert review and approval, and persist all versions in a versioned repository with full traceability links.

potential_sources: Coupling between intake schema design and inference rule set: if intake form structure changes, heuristic rules may require revision. Mitigation: define intake schema as a stable contract; version rules independently.

Coupling between expert review process and downstream performance requirement consumers: approval workflow must not block real-time system operation. Mitigation: separate approval gate from active performance target deployment; use staged rollout.
Coupling between traceability matrix and change-control system: both must remain synchronized. Mitigation: single source of truth for version history; automated consistency checks.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D balances automation (inference) with human oversight (expert review), reducing risk of incorrect derivations while maintaining scalability. Structured intake decouples mission context capture from derivation logic. Versioned repository supports both traceability and baseline history requirements. Hybrid approach avoids over-reliance on either pure manual (DP-C, slower) or pure algorithmic (DP-B, less transparent) solutions.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Domain experts are available and willing to review and approve inferred targets within acceptable time windows. Risk: bottleneck if review capacity is limited. Mitigation: define SLA for review; provide decision-support tools to accelerate approval.

Assumption: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured intake form without loss of critical nuance. Risk: oversimplification of complex operational scenarios. Mitigation: include free-text fields and expert annotation capability; validate intake completeness before inference.

Assumption: Inference heuristics can be formalized and maintained as the worksite mission evolves. Risk: rules become stale or inconsistent. Mitigation: periodic rule audit; version rules alongside mission context snapshots.

Assumption: Traceability matrix and version history can be maintained without significant manual overhead. Risk: documentation debt if process is not automated. Mitigation: integrate versioning into intake and approval workflows; automate traceability link generation.

Assumption: Safety plan and operational policies are stable inputs. Risk: if policies change frequently, performance targets may become misaligned. Mitigation: establish policy change notification process; trigger re-derivation when policies change.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005.1 is well-formed and maps cleanly to DP-D. Recommend clarifying acceptance criteria to specify: (1) minimum traceability granularity (e.g., each performance target must link to at least one worksite input), (2) baseline snapshot frequency (e.g., on policy change, quarterly review, or on-demand), (3) expert review SLA (e.g., approval within 5 business days), and (4) definition of 'documented set' (e.g., format, storage location, access control). These clarifications will strengthen the IM-layer design and reduce ambiguity in module ownership and interface contracts.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized intake, inference, expert review, and versioned repository for mission context capture, candidate target proposal, approval workflows, and full traceability management.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive mission context, operational parameters, and intake form submissions from external tasking systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Consume telemetry data to inform domain-specific heuristics; emit candidate target proposals and inference results.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emit safety-relevant alerts if inference or approval workflows detect constraint violations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicate versioned repository state, traceability links, and expert review requests across distributed nodes if required.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit. NGHE Solution is explicitly designed for intake, inference, expert review, and versioned persistence. Structured forms, heuristic application, approval workflows, and traceability are core to its domain. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary; internal logic remains cohesive.

Poor fit. Vehicle is a physical/operational platform, not a decision-support or data-persistence layer. Intake forms, heuristic inference, expert review workflows, and versioned repositories are orthogonal to vehicle control and state. High diffusion: would require Vehicle to host non-vehicle-specific logic, violating separation of concerns.

Poor fit. Teleoperation is a human-in-the-loop control interface, not a mission planning or inference engine. Intake forms and versioned repositories belong upstream of teleoperation. High diffusion: would couple teleoperation UI to backend inference and persistence, creating tight coupling and poor modularity.

Weak fit. Safety Services enforces constraints and alerts, but does not own intake, inference, expert review, or versioned persistence. The DP includes safety-relevant logic (expert approval, constraint checking), but the bulk of intake, heuristic inference, and repository management is outside Safety Services' scope. High diffusion: would require Safety Services to host non-safety-specific decision logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP is fundamentally a mission-planning and inference-support capability: structured intake, domain-specific reasoning, expert validation, and auditable persistence. These are core functions of a Next-Generation Hybrid Engine (NGHE) solution. The DP exhibits strong cohesion within NGHE Solution and minimal diffusion across other modules. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not require the DP logic to leak into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.
containment_risks: If expert review workflows require real-time human interaction via Teleoperation, there is a risk of tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation. Mitigation: define expert review as an asynchronous, out-of-band process with clear request/response interfaces.

If versioned repository must synchronize across distributed Vehicle instances, Network Link complexity may increase. Mitigation: centralize repository in NGHE Solution; use Network Link only for query/update transactions, not for full state replication.

If Safety Services must veto candidate targets in real-time, there is a risk of circular dependency (NGHE Solution -> SafetyAlert -> Safety Services -> NGHE Solution). Mitigation: Safety Services consumes SafetyAlert as a feed; NGHE Solution does not wait for Safety Services approval before persisting candidates (approval is a separate workflow step).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is explicitly scoped to intake, inference, expert review, and versioned repository management. Acceptance criteria require 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates'—both are directly realizable within NGHE Solution's versioned repository and traceability links. Verification can be performed via audit logs, repository snapshots, and traceability chain inspection.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR-NGHE-005.1 (derive performance requirements from worksite mission context) maps unambiguously to NGHE Solution's intake-and-inference architecture. The IM boundary owns the full lifecycle: capture (structured forms), reasoning (domain-specific heuristics), validation (expert review), and persistence (versioned repository with traceability). No ambiguity regarding which module is responsible for each sub-function.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP (hybrid intake-and-inference architecture) directly addresses the FR (derive performance requirements from worksite mission context). The DP specifies a complete solution path: structured intake forms capture mission context, domain-specific heuristics propose candidates, expert review ensures validity, and versioned persistence provides traceability. The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe implementation technology) and logically sound. It satisfies both explicit acceptance criteria: (1) documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs (via intake forms + heuristics + expert approval + repository), and (2) history of baselines/calibration updates (via versioned repository).

diffusion_risks: Expert review workflows may require synchronous human interaction via Teleoperation interface. Risk: tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation, blurring separation of concerns. Mitigation: enforce asynchronous, out-of-band expert review (e.g., web portal, email approval) with clear request/response message contracts. NGHE Solution must not block inference on Teleoperation availability.

Versioned repository synchronization across distributed Vehicle instances may create hidden complexity in Network Link. Risk: repository consistency logic leaks into network layer, increasing coupling. Mitigation: centralize repository in NGHE Solution; use Network Link only for transactional queries and updates, not for state replication. Define clear API contracts for repository access.

Safety Services may need to veto or modify candidate targets in real-time, creating potential circular dependency (NGHE Solution -> SafetyAlert -> Safety Services -> NGHE Solution feedback loop). Risk: deadlock or undefined ordering in approval workflows. Mitigation: Safety Services consumes SafetyAlert as a unidirectional feed; NGHE Solution persists candidates independently of Safety Services veto. Expert review is the approval gate, not Safety Services. Safety Services acts as a constraint checker post-approval.

Worksite safety plan and operational policies (constraint input) may be embedded in heuristics rather than externalized as configuration. Risk: policy changes require code modification, reducing agility. Mitigation: externalize safety policies and operational constraints as a separate configuration artifact consumed by NGHE Solution's heuristic engine.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment within NGHE Solution (0.92 score). The DP is fundamentally a mission-planning and inference-support capability—structured intake, domain-specific reasoning, expert validation, and auditable persistence—all core to a Next-Generation Hybrid Engine. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are correctly excluded; they are consumers of NGHE Solution outputs, not owners of its logic. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not require DP logic to diffuse into other modules. Minimal diffusion (0.08 score) indicates strong architectural cohesion.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define expert review as an asynchronous, out-of-band process with clear request/response interfaces. Add a constraint: 'Expert review shall not block inference or persistence; approval is a separate workflow step with defined SLAs.'

Externalize worksite safety plan and operational policies as a configuration artifact (e.g., JSON schema, policy rules engine) consumed by NGHE Solution's heuristic engine. This decouples policy updates from code changes and improves traceability to 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' constraint.

Define Network Link interface contract explicitly: specify transactional semantics (query, update, version fetch) rather than full state replication. Add a constraint: 'Repository synchronization shall use eventual consistency with conflict resolution rules defined in NGHE Solution.'

Add a constraint to clarify Safety Services interaction: 'Safety Services consumes SafetyAlert as a unidirectional feed and does not block NGHE Solution approval workflows. Safety constraint violations are logged and escalated post-approval.'

Extend acceptance criteria to include: 'All performance targets and their derivation chains (worksite input -> heuristic inference -> expert approval) shall be queryable and auditable via the versioned repository.' This makes traceability verification concrete.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is excellent (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions identify critical architectural clarifications that must be resolved before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. The primary concerns are: (1) expert review workflow coupling to Teleoperation, (2) externalization of safety policies and operational constraints, (3) explicit Network Link interface contracts, and (4) Safety Services interaction semantics. These are not showstoppers but represent design debt that will propagate into sub-level FRs if left unresolved. Given high safety criticality, clarifying these constraints at the current level is essential to prevent rework downstream. Revising DP and IM now to incorporate these clarifications will yield cleaner, more traceable sub-FR decomposition.

revise.why: The DP (hybrid intake-and-inference architecture) is logically sound but lacks explicit specification of three critical design decisions: (1) expert review shall be asynchronous and out-of-band, with clear request/response contracts that do not block inference or persistence; (2) worksite safety plan and operational policies shall be externalized as a configuration artifact (not embedded in heuristics), enabling policy updates without code changes; (3) Safety Services interaction shall be unidirectional (SafetyAlert consumption only), with Safety Services acting as a post-approval constraint checker, not an approval gate. Revising the DP to explicitly incorporate these three design principles will eliminate diffusion risks and clarify the boundary between NGHE Solution's inference logic and external system interactions. This revision also strengthens traceability to the 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' constraint.

assumptions: Expert review is a human-in-the-loop process that can be decoupled from real-time inference via asynchronous messaging or web portal workflows.
Worksite safety policies and operational constraints can be externalized as structured configuration (e.g., JSON schema, rule engine) without loss of expressiveness.
Network Link supports transactional semantics (query, update, version fetch) with eventual consistency guarantees; full state replication is not required.
Safety Services operates as a constraint validator post-approval, not as an approval gate; this prevents circular dependencies and deadlock scenarios.
Versioned repository can be centralized in NGHE Solution without creating bottlenecks for distributed Vehicle instances; transactional access patterns are sufficient.
open_risks: Expert review SLA and latency: if asynchronous review introduces unacceptable delays in performance target derivation, synchronous coupling may become necessary. Mitigation: define SLA targets (e.g., 4-hour review turnaround) and monitor compliance; escalate to synchronous review only if SLA is breached.

Policy externalization complexity: if safety policies are highly interdependent or context-sensitive, externalization as configuration may become unwieldy. Mitigation: prototype policy rule engine with representative worksite constraints; validate expressiveness before full implementation.

Network Link consistency: eventual consistency may introduce stale reads or version conflicts in distributed Vehicle instances. Mitigation: define conflict resolution rules (e.g., last-write-wins, version vector clocks) and document acceptable staleness windows.

Safety Services veto latency: if Safety Services detects constraint violations post-approval, remediation may be delayed. Mitigation: Safety Services operates in parallel with expert review (not sequentially); violations are logged and escalated to human operators with defined escalation SLAs.

High safety criticality amplifies risk of policy externalization: if configuration is corrupted or misconfigured, safety constraints may be silently violated. Mitigation: implement configuration validation (schema validation, constraint checking) and audit logging; require change control for policy updates.
evidence_needed: Prototype asynchronous expert review workflow (e.g., web portal, email approval) with defined request/response message contracts; measure latency and user experience impact.
Design and validate policy rule engine with representative worksite safety constraints; demonstrate that externalized policies can express all required safety rules without loss of expressiveness.

Define Network Link interface contract explicitly: specify transactional semantics (query, update, version fetch), consistency guarantees (eventual consistency with conflict resolution), and acceptable staleness windows.
Clarify Safety Services interaction: document unidirectional SafetyAlert consumption, post-approval constraint checking, and escalation workflows for constraint violations.

Audit trail design: demonstrate that all performance targets, their derivation chains (worksite input -> heuristic inference -> expert approval), and policy changes are queryable and auditable via versioned repository.

Safety case evidence: demonstrate that externalized policies, asynchronous expert review, and post-approval Safety Services veto do not introduce new failure modes or reduce safety assurance compared to synchronous, embedded-policy baseline.
